description: Experimental condition: 0 = Flat (ROI stays constant over rounds), 1 = Increasing (ROI increases over rounds)
Agreement: 'I managed to learn which characteristics of companies (if any) had an impact on the ROI they generated' (Scale: -2=Strongly disagree to 2=Strongly agree)
Agreement: 'I understood how the characteristics of companies relate to their ROIs' (Scale: -2=Strongly disagree to 2=Strongly agree)
Agreement: 'I know which characteristics of companies affected their ROIs, and in which direction' (Scale: -2=Strongly disagree to 2=Strongly agree)
Confidence rating: 'A larger Number of Competitors had a NEGATIVE impact on the ROI' (Scale: 1=Not at all confident to 5=Extremely confident)
Confidence rating: 'A larger Market Size had a POSITIVE impact on the ROI' (Scale: 1=Not at all confident to 5=Extremely confident)
Confidence rating: 'A larger Company Valuation had a POSITIVE impact on the ROI' (Scale: 1=Not at all confident to 5=Extremely confident)

Perceived earning trajectory: 1 = 'The ROI tended to increase over time, independently of which company I decided to invest in', 2 = 'The ROI tended to stay constant over time, independently of which company I decided to invest in'
